claim: Agent architectures are evolving from rigid 'on rails' workflows to more flexible decision-making systems as LLM capabilities improve
actionable: Design agent systems with decision points and dynamic routing to leverage modern models like Gemini 3, rather than purely sequential workflows

claim: Memory systems are increasingly important in modern agent frameworks, allowing agents to learn and personalize over sessions
actionable: Implement persistent memory storage (markdown, JSON, vector databases) in your agent architecture to maintain context across user interactions

claim: Human-in-the-loop mechanisms dramatically improve agent reliability by allowing agents to ask clarifying questions before proceeding
actionable: Add decision nodes where agents can request user feedback or clarification rather than proceeding with potentially wrong assumptions

claim: Dynamic routing in agent graphs allows models to decide traversal paths rather than following predefined sequences
actionable: Use dynamic routing to let LLMs select which node/tool to invoke next based on task context, improving flexibility over hardcoded workflows
